reviews.review_text:
I can't play this game that i paid for. Every time I try to play, it asks me to login to Ubisoft Connect. Keep in mind that I am already logged in, and when I enter my password but it says account suspended. For weeks I have been in contact with customer support but as soon as they fix the issue, it comes right back. 

It is frustrating that i can't play the game because of some issue on their end. I would not recommend buying this game for you won't be able to play it

This game is amazing but it did take some research and trail and error to get it to run perfectly. Because of its age, it doesn't run well on new processors or graphics cards. First fix would be to download process lasso. When you start the game find the ac4 process in the process lasso program and disable hyperthreading and make sure it only uses physical cores. That will fix the stuttering, frame drops and crashes. Once that's done, disable the physx in the graphics option if your using a newer graphics card since that is no longer supported and that will fix graphics crashes. These are the only two things I had to do and the game has run flawless for me. As a side note, it runs well on the steam deck.

I won't be buying the new version, i already own this one, i should be able to get the newer edition for free or heavily discounted at the very least. It's literally the same game with a bit of a graphic update and you want us to pay twice the amount Gold edition of this game is worth?... I'll pass. Scummy and scammy. I'll stick to this version.

Great game, yes, but, because it keeps asking for the password at launch, at random times the Ubisoft account just gives up for no reason, which means Ubisoft connect gets temporarily suspended, meaning you can't play the dam game. Most times it was wait for an hour o so, right now its been about 3 days without being allowed to play. Same with FC3.
Remove UC requirement or at least that dumb verification that clearly is not needed since other games in UC don't have it.

Assassin’s Creed IV: Black Flag is a game that surprised me a lot. There are many improvements over the previous game, AC III, and it’s still worth playing even in 2026.

One thing I didn’t like, though, is the number of tailing missions. Sometimes it gets really annoying because it feels like every new mission has you following someone. Despite that, the game has some very memorable characters. My favorite was Blackbeard he was an absolute badass.

The story follows Edward Kenway’s dream of becoming a rich pirate, and later he learns about the Assassins and their Creed. It’s a great story overall. The ship combat is awesome. At first, it feels incredibly fun, although it can become a bit grindy and repetitive if you’re trying to fully upgrade your ship. Still, the naval combat is very enjoyable, and attacking enemy ships feels satisfying.

Hunting sharks and whales in the ocean is also engaging, and exploring islands while uncovering mysteries adds a lot to the experience. Edward Kenway is a very likable protagonist. He’s also connected to other major characters in the series, being the father of Haytham Kenway and the grandfather of Connor Kenway.

With the remake reportedly coming soon, it’s exciting to think that more people will get to experience this masterpiece with modern graphics and gameplay improvements.

9/10 one of the best Assassin’s Creed games ever made.

In my opinion, this is one of the best Assassins I've ever played. The story is brilliant and engaging, the locations are vibrant and detailed, there are many different missions, from hunting to exploration, and the ability to upgrade and customize the ship's appearance is a joy. Assassins are mentioned in the game, but I think the game is more about pirates, although there's plenty of stealth. The biggest drawback of all Ubisoft games is probably their launcher, which can let you play one day and then lock you out the next. Unfortunately, this was the case with Far Cry 3, but here I just changed the password once and everything worked fine, which is also a plus.

My rating: ✭✭✭✭✭

Good game. ♥♥♥♥♥♥ launcher that asks you to log in every time you play. 

If you don't want to run the risk of being locked out of your account after one failed login attempt just pirate it. UPlay and Ubisoft Connect out here ruining lives.

Their Ubisolt log in is ♥♥♥♥ to be honest when you trying to log in and play that assasin screen its already been downloaded because of the ubisoft issue you can't play it on offline what a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ ass game that you going to purchased and you gonna donwload then in the end on the day you need to have online to play it and 2nd they also been having error that you need to re log in online back and back again they cannot even consider the steam as your system log in
From playing is as a kid in 2015 to playing it on PC now, I can gladly say that the game didn't disappoint from the sailing to fighting, parkour, in game experience overall a beauty.

this game was really good and a great story but the tailing missions were too repetitive but overall great game, great main character and good graphics for a game released in 2013 and the pirate idea was a fun one.

Ubisoft being Ubisoft. Honestly, just pirate the game to be fair. You can’t even play the Freedom Cry DLC here, they force you to buy it separately.

Assassin's Creed: Black Flag is one of the best entries in the series.

The PC version in 2026 has a few issues to note (there is a remake coming out, as such it may be better to wait for that unless you want to go back and experience the original), those include the achievement list including Freedom Cry achievements, the DLC of which is only available if you purchase the "Gold Edition" of the game on Steam, additionally there are 8 Multiplayer-specific achievements, while 1 of them can be done in singleplayer without too much hassle, the rest require coordinating with others if you really want to get them.

Technically, make sure to turn off the PhysX effects, because even with a modern NVIDIA GPU, this aspect is no longer maintained by NVIDIA since they removed 32-bit instructions from their firmware updates as of 2026. You can likely run the game maxed out for everything except that option, and get a reliable 60FPS out of it.

The "Kenway Fleet" aspect is also bugged, where if you wish to resolve it, you will need to grab a fan-made edit of the main executable to get it working again.

It's ultimately a shame that Ubisoft never cleaned up this version of the game, short of enabling Steam Achievements, as otherwise it would be a game worth checking out, but currently while it's still good, if you want to get the most out of it, you will have to put in some work on your own.

AC Black Flag one of my fav. AC's, however I can't recommend this game to anyone who plays on Winslop  10/11 PC's because of Ubislop DRM (Ubislop Connect). Over many tries I got black screens caused by the DRM, deleted save files because of DRM via Ubislop Connect sync errors, crashes whenever I want to tab out of the game which is now only possible via opening the steam overlay and then tapping out of the game! Trying to complete all achievements is impossible without Cheat-Engine because of the dead multiplayer, which also causes the game to crash because of Ubislop DRM, since it's DRM Ubislop Connect also acts as and intrusive kernel level Anti-Cheat Software!

My recommendation to play games in general is:
1. ditch Microslop Winslop
2. switch to Linux (SteamDeck/SteamOS, CachyOS or Nobara)
3. enjoy the game like you used to do!

Question: Should you buy the remake?
I believe as long as Ubislop insists on using Ubislop Connect as it's DRM I can't recommend anyone to buy anything from Ubislop! If you want them to treat you like the customer you are then you should voice your support for SKG (Stop Killing Games)!

I was excited to play this game -- clicked play and was prompted to copy/paste my license key for some reason, did that, and then to log in to my ubisoft account. The form showed a *noneditable* email field with an email i haven't used in years, and a link for login issues, which routed to a list of pages all about account bans and stolen accounts. Tried logging in on the web to change my email address there, and got an error -- "Oops! we couldn't log you in --we're here to help" routing again to the same page of links. 

Imagine if every seller on Amazon made you get an account on their site to purchase with them even when buying through Amazon--it would be a mess. For some reason it's tolerated in gaming despite creating nothing but friction and hassle, with promises of benefits that never materialize. I've refunded this game, and hopefully learned this lesson for the last time.
The external launcher is the worst thing that could have happened to this franchise; it makes you not want to buy the game.

A very fine AC game and a very fine pirate game as well. Atmosphere and gameplay is all good.

Probably to this day the best Single Player Pirate game. And one of the best Assassin's Creed games too.

This game is the best of both worlds, pirate x assassin. I love it, despite all its jank.

Movement can be fluid, but it's as if the game is trying really hard to determine where you want to go next while you're sprinting and climbing through terrain, and sometimes it picks left when you wanted to go right.

Combat is similar to the Rocksteady Batman games, made even easier but just as satisfying, taking out hoards of men on their own boat or stealthily wiping out an entire island.

Seriously, the entire experience when you're on the water, sinking ships and singing shanties, it's absolute cinema.

Such an incredible game, and I have to give it a negative review. Every single time I wanted to play, I had to create a new password because Ubisoft Connect kept saying “service unavailable,” and sometimes even “account suspended” due to repeated login attempts.

I also couldn’t use my preferred controller or headphones because, for some reason, they caused the game to crash. The only way to access the game was by creating a new password every single time I wanted to play.

In my opinion, this is one of the best games of all time.  This is the game that got me into Assassin's Creed games.  I am excited to see the remake.  The boating sequences never got any better than this game though.

crystalline, bone-crushing, dizzying magnificence. an absolute, uncompromising masterpiece elevated to its ultimate form. MY GLORIOUS KING EDWARD KENWAY is the sexiest, most charismatic, and most charming man in the history of this universe. the dopamine levels while playing go far beyond the limits of physical survival. every second is an aesthetic orgasm. the ideal of masculine beauty, charisma, and audacity. the greatest thing humanity has ever created. a game worth living for and worth dying for.

The NPCs in this AC are perfect. Just the right amount of challenge, but are mostly hilariously bad (in a good way). I remember spending so much time swimming around to get soldiers to shoot themselves. I am sure I could play this game all the way through another 5 times until I get fully burned out. If you have not tried AC, give this one a go. I am sure most will find this game fun. Sure the "hacking" abstergo missions are well... chores, but they are pretty short and do not take away too much from being a pirate. 11/10.

Came back to the game after the remake reveal to give it another go after about five years and it still holds up really well. It has some aging issues like the admittedly very easy combat and being kind of an open-world collectathon, but it still feels very good to play and the pirate/Assassin fantasy remains very strong. Edward is a very captivating protag and the story is as timeless as ever, with far more depth as a true Assassin's Creed game and not just as "a pirate game" than one might think on a first playthrough. I don't think you're losing too much by picking up AC4 during a Steam sale over waiting for BF:Resynced, this game is a true classic in every sense of the word and has the kind of heart and soul that isn't easily replicated.

Assassin’s Creed IV Black Flag:

Is easily one of the best Assassin’s Creed games, and honestly one of the best pirate games ever made.

The story follows Edward Kenway during the Golden Age of Piracy, and the Caribbean setting is amazing. Sailing across the ocean, upgrading the Jackdaw, hunting ships, exploring islands, diving for treasure, and getting pulled into the Assassin and Templar conflict all works so well together. It feels like a pirate game first and an Assassin’s Creed game second, and that is a big reason why it stands out.

The gameplay loop is addictive. You can do main missions, naval battles, forts, ship upgrades, hunting, collectables, underwater areas, and side activities without it feeling boring. The music and sea shanties also add so much atmosphere that even just sailing around can feel relaxing and fun.

The story, characters, combat, world, and exploration are all great. Edward is a strong main character, and the game does a great job making his journey feel meaningful. Going for 100% completion is mostly fun, although it can still get grindy in places, especially if you are clearing everything on the map.

I would recommend Assassin’s Creed IV Black Flag to almost anyone. Fans of the series, casual players, story players, pirate fans, and achievement hunters should all give it a try.

This is not just a good Assassin’s Creed game. It is a must-play.

Assassin’s Creed IV: Black Flag is easily one of my favorite Assassin’s Creed games. The Caribbean setting is absolutely beautiful and gives a real sense of adventure. Sailing across the sea, discovering islands, singing shanties with the crew, and getting into massive naval battles never gets old. Boarding enemy ships is still one of the most satisfying mechanics in the series.

What impressed me the most was the atmosphere — dynamic weather, huge storms, giant waves, and the feeling of being lost in the open sea. Sometimes I spent hours just sailing around and enjoying the world.

I've always been a fan of this franchise, probably my favorite.  And I was to see that the first time I bought this I couldn't play it because the controler didn't properly work whit the game; but they fixed it!!!  So now I can replay it!  Sadly, not as fun the second time around, but still; it's a great game!

Recently played again because news about the remake. I cant really say its the best pirate game because haven’t played a lot of pirate themed games, but the gameplay still feels really fun and nostalgic.

The naval battles are easily my favourite. Fighting other ships, shooting cannons, then jumping to the enemy ship for melee combat is top notch.

The world building, exploration and pirate atmosphere are also amazing, even if you just wonder around the sea while listening to sea shanties. Some of the collectible are also quite rewarding, like armor that can block bullets other than just another basic armor with a new design.

The story is worth replaying even though there are quite a lot of tailing missions, but hey, being and assassin’s is not always about killing.

My personal rating is 8/10 (8.5/10 because the naval battles are my favorite part of the game).

A Christmas Memory I’ll Never Forget

I still remember being a little kid, begging my parents for an Xbox 360 and Assassin’s Creed IV: Black Flag. I don’t even remember exactly when it released back then — all I knew was that I wanted it more than anything.

Then Christmas finally came.

I opened my presents and saw the Xbox 360 and Black Flag sitting there. I completely lost my mind. I was screaming, shaking, and ran straight to my room to set everything up as fast as possible. The second I opened that Black Flag disc case and heard the game start up on the screen, it felt magical.

I went back downstairs to finish opening presents, but honestly? My mind was already in the Caribbean sailing with Edward Kenway.

After everything was done, I carried all my gifts back to my room and played for the entire night until it got dark outside. I was completely addicted from the first moments. The atmosphere, the music, the ocean, the pirate life — nothing felt like it. Back then I couldn’t believe how insane the graphics looked, and honestly even today the game still holds up incredibly well. Running it now on my RTX 5080 feels surreal compared to when I first played it on the 360.

Seeing Ubisoft revisit Black Flag again years later hit me hard with nostalgia. I still have the original disc my parents bought me sitting safely on my shelf like a priceless gem. I’ve even got an Edward Kenway figure and Black Flag pirate flags in my room because this game genuinely became part of my childhood.

Some games are just games.

But Black Flag became a memory.
A Christmas memory I’ll probably carry forever.

10/10 — still one of the greatest Assassin’s Creed games ever made.

For a classic, this sure feels a lot more fun than most of the games that I've played over the years. Great story that makes you actually feel something rather than just taking nostalgia using that to take your time. If you choose to play this game, I encourage you to play it to the fullest. I'll see you out on the Caribbean, pirate.

Great pirate game. but could have been better if it wasn't rushed to release in under a year from the previous game. After Desmond and the modern day storyline were killed in the previous years entry (AC3), the story felt unfinished in Black Flag because of it. Being that AC3 and black flag were developed simultaneously by different teams, they likely had to make some last minute decisions for the story and the modern day segments of having you work for Abstergo this time, and also asset flipping and needing to make the game revolve around a similar time period and lineage to AC3's. 

By the time this game dropped in 2013, it was the second (third if you count liberation) attempt at a revamp of the engine the series was running on. 2012's AC3 was the first attempt, and streamlined the controls and combat to be more fluid with the press of a button, instead of having different contexts for actions by holding the right trigger, as an example from the previous games. 

This sort of made AC3 a bit of a chore to replay at times because of how easy it was, but also because of just where it brought the series in general. Constant revamps to the core gameplay and such.  

Given how much the series resembles an RPG now, this game is still not too much of a change from AC1-Revelations and its gameplay, but it was enough to turn me off. Some other good things about this one though is that the world is vibrant and beautiful, and while the story is pretty forgettable, partly due to how the pacing is at some parts (Some characters you may not remember in later parts), it's not bad per say. It actually reminded me a lot of the first game in terms of how mysterious its atmosphere was, and the way the modern day segments were akin to the segments from that game. I think it's a better attempt at the modern day story after Desmond than what Origins tried to do. 

Another issue with the story is that it doesn't really involve, or do much of anything new for the Assassin's Order, for the majority of the game. I do like what segments there are, but some of the characters can feel forced into it at points. There also really isn't much significance added to the plot, that wasn't already established during the American Revolution with AC3, which doesn't take place much long after this game. Having you play as Connor's grandfather seems like an odd choice, but I liked Edwards story overall and think it works pretty well for a game where you play as a pirate/privateer.

The modern day segments do a decent job of providing a significant amount of lore about the animus and the subjects, stuff which hadn't been seen or talked about before, but it's not much fun having to seek out and read all of the lore  behind pages of text and hacking mini games. It just feels like a cheap alternative to giving us a game that could have played some of the modern day events and such. 

Watch Dogs could have been that, given its similarities to Assassin's Creed, but given the direction this series was supposedly meant to to take with the modern day segments, I feel like this game was nothing more than a filler for those scrapped ideas. I do think they did a good job with what left of the story that had to work with after AC3.

At the end of the day, most money Ubisoft makes from this series is because it's an action adventure game set during different times periods. Almost like an alternate universe historical simulator in some regards.... I'm not surprised they haven't made a fully modern day game yet. I still wouldn't think it'd work as well without Desmond, though.

I do like the ship combat as well, but it wasn't really anything new given AC3 also had the same ship combat a year prior. It sort of gets tiring after a while when you have to grind to upgrade the ship to play main missions that are mostly tailing other ships and such. I feel like if you grew up enjoying the gameplay loop of AC1-Revelations (3 to an extent), you might have found this game somewhat repetitive at the time of its release. 

it's a pretty good game, but it's not my favorite AC game.

Around 24 hours of playing this game and completing the main story I got to say this game is peak and was ahead of its time in 2013. For starters the movement is so good to the point where its too good by which I mean you cant climb straight up without free running up on any ledge. The combat is good the combos you can do are very clean. Ship combat is good too only problem is getting railed by multiple Man O Wars, thankfully you can board the ships once there damaged enough. The assassinations are fun especially when you progress in the story and get the rope dart. The weapons you get in the game are so fun to use I liked crafting all the gun holsters to carry more guns and quick firing with them its so clean and helps you evade hits if your low on health. The most important part about this game is pirating. Finding maps and going to there location is a very good way of getting money. Hunting in the game is simple for land creatures nothing too special, but hunting the sea life creatures is so fun probably my favorite side activity. Gaining money is satisfying mainly is early game because the better weapons and ship upgrades makes battling a tad easier. I personally don't like using the diving bell because of my thalassophobia I know I have a fear of the ocean and I'm playing a game intended for sailing in the ocean, but the game is just too good. Battling the legendary ships had me rethinking life, but its worth the money and the achievement for the flex. The side missions are fun to do but they get repetitive, but I cant say I don't like doing them. The collectables are very time consuming but if you want cooler outfits or outfits with buffs like the Mayan outfit its worth the time. Last thing are the animus missions some people might disagree but I hate them there so useless the only way I would do the activates in the hub would be for the trophies. All in conclusion Assassin's Creed Black Flag is such a great and unique game and I cant wait for the Resynced Remake totally worth the money.

Sailing the seas as Edward Kenway feels as good as prowling the city as Ezio.

This game oozes fun and interesting takes on the events of the Golden Age of Piracy in the Caribbean. I love the settings like Nassau, Havana, and all the little unnamed islands sprawled about a huge map. The events and ships that you run into as you sail along, listening to the shanties that your crewmates sing, make the world feel lived in and believable. 

As with the other games, the modern-day missions feel flat and kind of uninteresting, aside from the take of Abstergo and [spoiler] their entertainment enterprise as they monetize the Animus [/spoiler]! Some familiar faces show up, but don't really have any real bearing in the game's story. You do get some lore about previous events, but then you forget about all those immediately as you dive back into your Animus and set sail again. It's definitely a good thing Ubisoft decided to make this part of the game as small as possible.

Overall, Edward is up there for me with all the assassins previous to this game (yes, even Connor!).

If you're looking for some pirate fun with the flavor of Assassin's Creed sprinkled in, I think this entry in the series definitely delivers.

I've played the later entries and I feel like AC has lost it's way. It doesn't have the soul, the passion, as it once had with these earlier titles. I'm glad they're going back and doing Resync for Edward (and here's hoping a modern remaster for AC2 trilogy!), but I want more original and fun ideas from the series. 

Dev's, if you're seeing this, please use this entry as your inspiration! If you can cut through the corporate yellow tape, bring some fresh ideas to the table!

Overall, 10/10 would sail into Davy's Jones' locker and back to play this game for the first time again!
Wonderful game and the last AC with some substance to it. The movement can get very clunky/frustrating at points, but the sailing makes up for it.

Good:
-Pretty good gameplay
-Pretty good story
-Lots of interesting things to do in the open world

Bad:
-Tailing missions sucks balls

Conclusion:
A Pretty good game 8/10.
i freaking love this game. been playing since the 360 days of release. cant wait for resync to release. also remember that Edward is Zaddy fr.

With Resynced coming out, I wanted to try and actually play through this and see why people love this entry so much. I’ve tried to play Black Flag multiple times in the past and always stopped, mainly because I didn’t like the sailing sections, I use to think they were the worst part of the game but after 30+ hrs I get why this game is loved so much.

Edward is completely different from any other protagonist in the AC franchise. Going from typical pirate just wanting a better life for himself, to understanding the creed and wanting to live by it and redeem his past actions. He’s up there with Ezio and Arno as the most interesting characters in AC franchise has.

The setting is probably one of my favourites so far next to France in Unity. Everything is just gorgeous to look at, bright and colourful, the weather patterns while you’re at sea can change from peaceful to storms in seconds which gives the sailing sections a bit more of a challenge, as you’re avoiding harsher waves and waterspouts, god help you if you’re doing that while shooting at other ships too!

Gameplay is standard, if you played an AC game from 2 onwards then it’s no different really. Still a bit of jank while doing parkour, especially if you’re jumping into trees, but it’s enjoyable. I still think Unity has the best parkour in the franchise, but you can tell they worked on it a bit more in Black Flag.

I was dreading the sailing sections, but after a bit of time I started enjoying them. I do think they slow the game down a lot, and it beats the whole purpose of Assassin’s and stealth, but considering you’re mainly a pirate it makes sense. Just slows things down a bit too much for my liking, but they are fun.

I’d say anyone who’s interested in this game or Resynced, definitely pick this up. If you can go for all the DLC too. Freedom Cry is a good expansion to the game (didn’t care too much for the protagonist thought) but Aveline is just odd… I don’t understand what this had to do with anything in Black Flag other than slavery? Too short and Aveline isn’t really that interesting so you can skip that one.
My rating: 8/10.

Black Flag is really a one of a kind game in the AAA market. It fulfills the pirate fantasy that no other game emulates. The story is one of piracy and also connects to the larger Assassin's Creed mythos. I enjoyed both the modern day and the past storylines. Edward's story is very well written and acted while I enjoyed seeing the aftermath of AC3's modern day ending. Gameplay wise the game focuses on innovating the naval combat while keeping the other elements mostly the same from previous games. Overall Black Flag is a good Assassin's Creed game while simultaneously having a pirate fantasy being fulfilled.
Great game - a classic. Looking forward to the resync

I tried the first Assassin's Creed when it came out on PS3. I wanted to like it. The atmosphere and story were very intriguing to me. But I just couldn't get over the movement. Years later, I hear Black Flag is the best one so I try again. Same crappy movement. I don't how these games' movement got praised so much over the years. Ubisoft very clearly focused on making it look good, NOT feel good. The character ignores directional input and just does what they want. See a wall you want to jump to forward and to the left? You can tilt your stick that way but he's gonna jump on the post to the right because it's cooler looking or closer or who knows what backwards logic it's using. The amount of times the character just doesn't do what I'm inputting is staggering to the point that I don't know how it got past play testing. Try to go forward on the very obvious path in front of me? Nah he jumps off the cliff. Try to jump anywhere I want him to jump? Nah the game is going to literally ignore the direction you pushed and just make assumptions. The movement alone frustrates me to the point of not recommending this game, or the entire series for that matter, and I haven't even mentioned having to create an online account for a single player game. Classic Ubisoft and their infinite wisdom. Took an awesome story/atmosphere and just spit all over it.
The game is good, the fact you can't play it without Ubisoft connect is the big downside, would not recommend

A few years ago, Ubisoft updated their launcher so that "legacy" games (basically anything released before 2017) will need you to log in each and every time they play. They have made it clear this was a deliberate move on their part and Assassin's Creed IV is affected as a result. **BY THE WAY THIS IS NOT JUST A STEAM ISSUE IT AFFECTS GAMES BOUGHT AND INSTALLED AND PLAYED ON UBISOFT CONNECT.**

I have some very controversial opinions on this game that I would've given a thumbs down regardless (I think it's the worst 360-era Assassin's Creed to play, but one with enough promise that I hope the remake amends its many issues) but for this malarkey alone, stay away!
